events.title: Roswell Incident
events.detail: US Army Air Forces recovers debris near Roswell, NM. Initial press release says 'flying disc.' Hours later retracted to 'weather balloon.' In 1994, USAF acknowledged the debris was from Project Mogul (nuclear test monitoring). In 1997, USAF report attributes 'alien body' reports to parachute test dummies.

events.title: Project Sign Established
events.detail: US Air Force launches Project Sign, its first official UFO investigation program. The project's classified 'Estimate of the Situation' reportedly concluded some UFOs were interplanetary — the report was ordered destroyed by USAF Chief of Staff Gen. Hoyt Vandenberg.

events.title: Project Blue Book Begins
events.detail: USAF replaces Project Grudge with Project Blue Book, which will investigate 12,618 UFO reports over 17 years. Run from Wright-Patterson AFB. Astronomer J. Allen Hynek serves as scientific consultant.

events.title: Washington D.C. UFO Incidents
events.detail: Multiple unidentified objects detected on radar at Washington National Airport and Andrews AFB over two consecutive weekends in July 1952. USAF jets scrambled. Objects tracked at speeds up to 7,000 mph. Largest press conference since WWII held by the Pentagon to address the sightings.

events.title: CIA Robertson Panel
events.detail: CIA convenes the Robertson Panel of scientists to review UFO evidence. Panel recommends debunking and public education campaign to reduce public interest in UFOs, citing national security concerns about clogging military channels with false reports.

events.title: Condon Report Published
events.detail: University of Colorado publishes the Condon Report, concluding that UFO study has not contributed to scientific knowledge and recommending termination of Project Blue Book. Critics note the report's own data showed ~30% of cases remained unexplained.

events.title: Project Blue Book Closed
events.detail: USAF closes Project Blue Book after 17 years and 12,618 investigations. 701 cases (5.5%) classified as 'unidentified.' USAF concludes: no UFO investigated was a threat to national security, no evidence of extraterrestrial technology. This ends all publicly acknowledged US government UFO investigation for decades.

events.title: FOIA Releases CIA UFO Files
events.detail: Following FOIA lawsuits, the CIA releases hundreds of previously classified UFO-related documents, revealing ongoing intelligence interest in UFOs despite public denials. Documents show CIA monitored UFO reports worldwide during the Cold War.

events.title: Rendlesham Forest Incident
events.detail: US Air Force personnel at RAF Woodbridge (UK) report close encounter with unidentified craft over multiple nights in December. Lt. Col. Charles Halt files official report (Halt Memo) to UK Ministry of Defence. Multiple military witnesses provide testimony. MOD investigated but concluded no threat to national security.

events.title: Belgian UFO Wave
events.detail: Thousands of witnesses including Belgian police report large triangular craft over Belgium. Belgian Air Force scrambles F-16s; radar confirms unknown targets. Belgian military holds unprecedented press conference acknowledging the sightings and releasing radar data. Over 2,000 reports collected.

events.title: Phoenix Lights
events.detail: Thousands of witnesses across Arizona report massive V-shaped formation of lights. Governor Fife Symington initially mocks the reports but later admits he also witnessed the phenomenon and calls it 'otherworldly.' USAF attributes some sightings to A-10 flare drops; explanation does not account for all reports.

events.title: USS Nimitz 'Tic Tac' Encounter
events.detail: Navy pilots from USS Nimitz carrier group encounter an anomalous aerial object ('Tic Tac') off San Diego coast during training exercises. Cmdr. David Fravor and Lt. Cmdr. Alex Dietrich observe object exhibiting no visible propulsion, no wings, no exhaust. Onboard systems (FLIR, radar, SPY-1) track the object. Pentagon later confirms the video (FLIR1) is authentic. Multiple witnesses across multiple platforms.

events.title: AATIP Program Created
events.detail: The Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP) is created within the Defense Intelligence Agency at the request of Sen. Harry Reid, with $22 million in funding secured through the DIA budget. Program investigates military UAP encounters. Run by Luis Elizondo from the Office of the Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence.

events.title: USS Roosevelt 'Gimbal' & 'GoFast' Encounters
events.detail: Navy pilots from USS Theodore Roosevelt encounter anomalous objects daily during training exercises off the US East Coast from 2014 to 2015. Two key infrared videos — 'Gimbal' and 'GoFast' — are recorded. Lt. Ryan Graves reports near-daily sightings. Pentagon later confirms both videos are authentic unclassified footage.

events.title: NY Times Reveals AATIP Program
events.detail: New York Times publishes groundbreaking story revealing the existence of the Pentagon's secret $22M AATIP program. Article includes declassified Navy encounter videos (FLIR1, Gimbal, GoFast). Luis Elizondo confirmed as former program director who resigned in protest over secrecy. To The Stars Academy (Tom DeLonge's organization) releases the videos publicly. Story triggers renewed mainstream interest in UAPs.

events.title: Navy Establishes UAP Reporting Guidelines
events.detail: US Navy formally establishes guidelines for pilots to report UAP encounters, reversing decades of stigma. Navy confirms the three leaked videos (FLIR1, Gimbal, GoFast) are authentic and show 'unidentified aerial phenomena.' This represents a major institutional shift — military officially acknowledging UAP as a real phenomenon requiring formal reporting.

events.title: Pentagon Officially Releases UAP Videos
events.detail: Pentagon formally releases three UAP videos (FLIR1, Gimbal, GoFast) to 'clear up any misconceptions by the public on whether or not the footage that has been circulating was real.' DoD spokesperson confirms the videos depict 'unidentified aerial phenomena.' This marks the first time the US government officially acknowledges and releases UAP footage.

events.title: UAP Task Force Established
events.detail: Deputy Secretary of Defense establishes the Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force (UAPTF) under the Office of Naval Intelligence (ONI) to detect, analyze, and catalog UAPs that could threaten US national security. First official DoD UAP investigation body since Project Blue Book ended in 1969.

events.title: ODNI Preliminary Assessment on UAP
events.detail: Office of the Director of National Intelligence releases classified/unclassified preliminary assessment on UAP to Congress. Report examines 144 incidents (2004–2021). Of those, only 1 was identified (deflating balloon). The 143 remaining cases were unresolved. Report states UAP 'clearly pose a safety of flight issue and may pose a challenge to U.S. national security.' Notably does not rule out extraterrestrial origin.

events.title: First Congressional UAP Hearing in 50+ Years
events.detail: House Intelligence Subcommittee holds first open congressional hearing on UAPs in over 50 years. Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence Ronald Moultrie and Deputy Director of Naval Intelligence Scott Bray testify. DoD shows previously unreleased UAP video. Witnesses acknowledge 400+ UAP reports from military personnel.

events.title: AARO Established by DoD
events.detail: DoD establishes the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) to replace the UAPTF. Led by Dr. Sean Kirkpatrick. AARO has broader mandate: investigate UAPs across all domains (air, sea, space, transmedium). Mandated by FY2022 NDAA. First permanent UAP investigation office with dedicated funding and interagency coordination authority.

events.title: Sol Foundation Established
events.detail: Stanford immunologist Dr. Garry Nolan and anthropologist Peter Skafish co-found the Sol Foundation, a think tank for academic and policy research on UAP. First symposium held November 2023 at Stanford with Grusch, Elizondo, and Colonel Karl Nell. Marks a shift toward mainstream academic engagement with UAP research.

events.title: David Grusch Goes Public as Whistleblower
events.detail: Former intelligence official David Grusch gives interview to NewsNation/The Debrief alleging the US government possesses retrieved non-human craft and biologics. Grusch filed a complaint with the Intelligence Community Inspector General (ICIG), who found it 'credible and urgent.' Grusch claims retaliation for his disclosures. He held Top Secret/SCI clearance and served on the UAP Task Force.

events.title: House Oversight UAP Hearing — Grusch Testifies Under Oath
events.detail: House Oversight Committee holds public hearing. David Grusch testifies under oath that the US has been recovering non-human craft for decades and has a 'multi-decade UAP crash retrieval program.' Navy pilots Ryan Graves and David Fravor also testify about their encounters. Grusch states he was told of 'non-human biologics' recovered from crash sites. Bipartisan interest — both Democratic and Republican members express alarm at alleged secrecy.

events.title: Schumer Introduces UAP Disclosure Act
events.detail: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer introduces the UAP Disclosure Act of 2023 as an amendment to the FY2024 NDAA. Bill would create a review board modeled on the JFK Assassination Records Act to declassify UAP records, establish eminent domain over any recovered materials, and mandate government disclosure within 25 years. Co-sponsored by Sen. Mike Rounds (R-SD). Bill passes Senate but key provisions stripped in House conference due to opposition from defense/intel committee leaders.

events.title: AARO Historical Report Volume I Released
events.detail: AARO releases Historical Record Report Volume I, covering US government investigation of UAP from 1945 to present. Report concludes: 'no empirical evidence' of extraterrestrial technology, no crash retrieval program, and no cover-up. Report states many UAP sightings are misidentified ordinary objects or classified programs. Whistleblowers and congressional UAP advocates strongly dispute the findings. ICIG previously found Grusch's claims 'credible and urgent' — contradicting AARO's conclusions.

events.title: AARO Director Kirkpatrick Resigns
events.detail: Dr. Sean Kirkpatrick resigns as founding director of AARO. In subsequent media appearances, Kirkpatrick states he found no evidence of extraterrestrial technology but criticizes Congress for being 'convinced by conspiracy theorists.' UAP advocates accuse Kirkpatrick of not having been granted access to the most classified programs. Tim Phillips named acting director.

events.title: Senate Armed Services UAP Hearing
events.detail: Senate Armed Services Committee holds hearing on UAP. Witnesses include DoD officials and intelligence community representatives. Committee members press for more disclosure and criticize pace of AARO's work. Multiple senators express frustration at being denied access to classified UAP programs. Hearing raises questions about whether AARO has been given full access to all relevant programs.

events.title: Langley AFB Drone Incursions — 17 Consecutive Nights
events.detail: Unknown drones penetrate restricted airspace over Langley Air Force Base for 17 consecutive nights. F-22 Raptors scrambled. Pentagon confirms incursions but cannot identify operators. Wall Street Journal breaks the story, reigniting debate over aerial intrusions at sensitive military sites.

events.title: UAP Disclosure Act Gutted in House Conference
events.detail: Schumer-Rounds UAP Disclosure Act stripped of eminent domain, subpoena power, and review board during FY2025 NDAA conference. Only recordkeeping provisions survive. Defense and intelligence committee leaders led the opposition, citing national security.

events.title: FY2025 NDAA UAP Provisions Signed
events.detail: FY2025 National Defense Authorization Act signed into law with UAP-related provisions including new whistleblower protections for UAP disclosures, expanded AARO authority, and requirements for additional reporting to Congress. Some elements of the original Schumer UAP Disclosure Act incorporated in weakened form.

events.title: AARO Historical Review Volume 2 — Overdue
events.detail: AARO Historical Record Report Volume 2 remains unreleased past its expected delivery date. Volume 1 drew heavy criticism for concluding 'no empirical evidence.' The delay fuels speculation about internal disagreements, particularly following Kirkpatrick's departure and change in AARO leadership.

events.title: Sol Foundation 2nd Annual Symposium
events.detail: Sol Foundation holds second annual symposium with expanded academic and government participation. Papers cover materials science, policy frameworks, and historical analysis. Growing institutional credibility signals mainstream academia's increasing engagement with UAP research.

events.title: Trump Signs UAP Disclosure Executive Order
events.detail: President Trump signs executive order directing the Pentagon to release UAP and alien-related files to the public, marking the first presidential directive specifically targeting UAP disclosure. Order mandates a 90-day review of all classified UAP programs with public release timeline. Trump states 'the American people deserve to know.' Advocates call it the most significant UAP disclosure action ever taken by a sitting president.
